name: The Embalse de Loriguilla — Circular from Pedralba
name_original: El Embalse de Loriguilla — Circular desde Pedralba
region_intro: Road cycling in Turia offers a diverse landscape for no traffic road cycling routes, characterized by its unique urban green spaces and extended natural parks. The region features the Turia Gardens, a 5.6 miles (9 km) traffic-free path within the city, and the broader Turia River Park, which extends for 15.5 miles (25 km) through Mediterranean forests and varied terrain. Beyond the urban core, the Turia Nature Park encompasses over 4,600 hectares, providing routes with significant elevation gains and diverse ecosystems. This combination allows for a range of cycling experiences, from flat urban rides to more challenging routes through natural landscapes. Best no traffic road cycling routes in Turia The most popular no traffic road cycling route is The Embalse de Loriguilla — Circular from Pedralba, a 44.0 miles (70.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the area around the Loriguilla reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Path to Barranco de Portaceli – Pla de Llit Recreation Area loop from Paterna, a moderate 30.5 miles (49.0 km) path. This route offers varied terrain, including sections through ravines and natural recreation areas. Local road cyclists also love the Antenas de Pedralba – El Gabaldón Pass loop from Pedralba, a 34.0 miles (54.8 km) trail leading through elevated areas with potential viewpoints, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Road cycling in Turia is defined by urban green corridors, Mediterranean forests, and geological formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from flat, traffic-free paths to routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Turia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 1,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Turia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic road cycling routes are available in Turia?
A: There are over a dozen dedicated no-traffic road cycling routes in Turia, ranging from moderate to difficult, offering diverse experiences away from vehicle traffic. These routes allow you to explore the region's natural beauty and cultural landmarks safely.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Turia's no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: Turia offers a varied cycling landscape. The urban &lt;a href="https://www.visitvalencia.com/en/what-to-do-valencia/city-routes/valencia-by-bike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turia Gardens&lt;/a&gt; provide mostly flat, traffic-free paths ideal for leisurely rides. As you venture into the broader Turia River Park and Turia Nature Park, you'll encounter more varied terrain, including some unpaved segments and significant elevation gains, especially on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e859904891/the-embalse-de-loriguilla-circular-from-pedralba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Embalse de Loriguilla — Circular from Pedralba&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along these routes?
A: Many routes traverse the beautiful Turia Gardens, offering views of exotic plants, fountains, and historic bridges. You might also pass by the futuristic City of Arts and Sciences. Further afield, within the Turia Nature Park, you can discover valuable Mediterranean woodlands, the Hoz ravine, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4058752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turia River Weir&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also lead to natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1085458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rodana Gran&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5569120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallesa Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular no-traffic road cycling routes in Turia?
A: Yes, many of the no-traffic road cycling routes in Turia are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e859904891/the-embalse-de-loriguilla-circular-from-pedralba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Embalse de Loriguilla — Circular from Pedralba&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 70.7 km loop, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39535185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matahombrez loop from Paterna&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 43 km ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for no-traffic road cycling in Turia?
A: Turia's mild climate makes it suitable for road cycling year-round. However, spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures for longer rides. Even in summer, the early mornings and late afternoons can be enjoyable, especially on routes that offer shade from Mediterranean forests.

Q: Are there options for more challenging no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38186317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojú Village – Castell de Benisanó loop from Entrepins&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains over its 127 km distance. These routes often venture into the more rugged parts of the Turia Nature Park, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about no-traffic cycling in Turia?
A: The komoot community highly rates the no-traffic road cycling routes in Turia, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the tranquility of the routes, the beautiful natural scenery, and the opportunity to cycle safely away from urban traffic. The diverse landscapes, from urban green spaces to Mediterranean forests, are frequently highlighted.

Q: Can I find amenities like cafes or rest stops along these routes?
A: While the urban sections, particularly around the Turia Gardens, offer numerous cafes and facilities, routes extending into the Turia Nature Park may have fewer amenities. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer or more remote routes, and plan your stops in advance.

Q: How can I access the starting points of these no-traffic routes?
A: Many routes are accessible from urban centers like Valencia, especially those starting near the Turia Gardens or Cabecera Park. For routes further afield, public transport options or parking facilities near the starting points are often available. The &lt;a href="https://www.visitvalencia.com/en/what-to-do-valencia/nature-in-valencia/turia-nature-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turia Nature Park&lt;/a&gt; website may offer specific guidance on access points.

Q: Are there any moderate-difficulty no-traffic road cycling routes suitable for a good day out?
A: Yes, Turia offers several moderate-difficulty routes perfect for a rewarding day of cycling. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31437606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Antenas de Pedralba – El Gabaldón Pass loop from Pedralba&lt;/a&gt; is a 54.7 km route with varied terrain, providing a good balance of challenge and enjoyment without being overly strenuous.
tips: A very picturesque town in the Valencian Community with many places of interest and activities in nature, among them the Charco Azul and several viewpoints. Very nice town to visit!

A very picturesque and quiet place. The views are very nice and can be reached by road in good condition. There is parking and from here you can start several routes that can end in Chulilla or Sot de Chera.
